description:
Parent epic fz-rh2.21; eli5.html is the design doc; requires fz-rh2.21.1-.4 landed. This ticket pins convergence so it can never silently regress. The original symptom: ./target/release/fz2 build -o /tmp/out --log-telemetry LOG fixtures/quicksort/input.fz was bimodal — ~332 jobs / 2.5MB log on lucky hash seeds, 54,000+ jobs / 700MB on unlucky ones (the engine ran a period-2 oscillation; see the epic). After fz-rh2.21.4, termination at the unique least fixpoint is a theorem (monotone joins + finite chains via widening + fair scheduling); this ticket turns the theorem's observable consequences into tests that use telemetry as the production seam (no test-only hooks).

PINS (TDD; all observe telemetry via Capture or event-time handlers):
1. Revision bound: drive the quicksort fixture (fixtures/quicksort/input.fz source, also available as the existing drive_test quicksort cases) to Resolved; attach a handler over fz.compiler2.work_graph.applied (or the return_type.defined events) recording per-fact revisions; assert every ReturnType fact's final revision is <= a small constant (start at 8; it should comfortably hold at 3-4). Assert total jobs ran is bounded (use the drive span's jobs_ran measurement; bound generously, e.g. < 2000 — the runaway was 54k).
2. Schedule independence: run the same in-process drive N=20 times (fresh World each iteration, same fixture) inside one test; assert the jobs_ran counts across iterations are ALL equal AND the final activation/closure shape is identical each time (capture SemanticClosed / closure executables count). Rationale: per-process RandomState made runs bimodal; in-process repetition with fresh HashMaps per World still varies iteration order via allocation addresses — if the design is right, counts are stable anyway; if this test flakes, the design has a hole and the test has found it (do NOT loosen the assertion without understanding the variance).
3. Self-wake quiescence: assert that after Resolved, re-driving with no new submissions runs zero jobs (pending_jobs == 0 and a second drive() returns Resolved immediately with jobs_ran == 0 via the drive span measurement).
4. Existing canaries stay pinned and are now load-bearing for this epic: compiler2_quicksort_root_closes_with_a_finite_recursive_frontier (exactly 2 append activations, bounded total), the redefinition suite (compiler2_redefining_main_retracts_the_old_root_frontier_and_activates_foo and the helper-redefinition minimal-rerun tests), and the deepening-recursion fixture from fz-rh2.21.4.
5. End-to-end smoke (manual-gated like the existing telemetry_dump tests, #[ignore] with a reason string): a test that shells the release binary 20x over fixtures/quicksort/input.fz with --log-telemetry to a temp file and asserts each log stays under a few MB and job counts match across runs. Mark ignored so CI cost stays controlled; it exists for humans to run when touching the engine.

Also: re-run the previously hanging/slow compiler2 drive tests (the quicksort family that ran >60s under cargo test pre-fix, e.g. compiler2_native_program_jit_runs_quicksort_through_compiler2_codegen, compiler2_backend_program_keeps_only_the_closed_quicksort_inventory) and record their post-fix wall time in the commit message — they were intermittently hostage to the oscillation; if any still runs >60s, investigate before closing (it would indicate a residual non-convergence or a genuine perf issue distinct from this epic — file a ticket with the evidence either way).

Acceptance: all pins green per epic baseline; zero warnings; one commit. This ticket closes Arc A.
title: convergence pinning: revision bounds, schedule independence, quiescence
